name: ABF
oneliner: Simplest long-context approach: increase RoPE base from 10,000 to 500K-1M during training. No post-hoc scaling needed.
explanation: Adjusted Base Frequency is the simplest way to extend a model's context window when using RoPE position encoding. Instead of the default base of 10,000, the base is increased to 500,000 or even 1,000,000, which stretches the position wavelengths so the model can represent longer sequences without confusion. Local token-to-token discrimination is preserved because the highest-frequency band is unaffected by the base change. This technique requires continued pre-training. Llama 3 uses a base of 500K, and Qwen 2 uses 1M.
fundamentals: $\theta_i$ = B^(-2i/d). Max wavelength = 2$\pi$·B. base=10K → ~63K positions; base=500K → ~3.1M positions; base=1M → ~6.3M positions. High-freq (i=0): $\theta_0$=1 always (base^0=1) — local info preserved for any base. Mathematically equivalent to NTK-aware with specific $\alpha$, but applied during training not post-hoc. The model learns to use the extended position range natively — no distributional mismatch.
sources: Meta, 'The Llama 3 Herd of Models,' 2024, arXiv:2407.21783
Xiong et al., 'Effective Long-Context Scaling,' 2023, arXiv:2309.16039
